Title: Natural variation in OsTPS8 confers differential regulation of chalkiness and seed vigor in indica and japonica rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Trehalose-6-phosphate synthase family, class II TPS
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in grains; higher in C50/IR24-haplotype grains from 9 DAF during grain filling; also expressed in embryo and embryonic roots during germination
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing grains during grain filling; embryo and embryonic roots during seed germination
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing grain/endosperm; mature grains; germinating seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signal linking carbon status to starch mobilization and seed germination; suppresses OsMYBS1 and α-amylase expression, thereby restraining starch degradation and chalkiness
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated OsTPS8 lowers Tre6P, promotes starch degradation, increases grain chalkiness, and enhances seed vigor/germination
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; transgenic complementation with native promoters; promoter-swap constructs; subcellular localization; yeast complementation; in vitro enzyme assay; Y2H; LCI; co-IP; OsTPS1 activity inhibition assay; metabolite profiling of Tre6P; CRISPR knockout/base editing; germination assays; CSSL validation
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsTPS8 promoters from ASO or C50 (inducible/native); pOsTPS8::OsTPS8-GFP (native); pCAMBIA1381Z GUS under OsTPS8 promoter; promoter base-edited endogenous OsTPS8
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard field conditions in Nanjing and Hainan; seed germination after imbibition at 30°C; developmental stage during grain filling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Single-gene association analysis in 331 Asian cultivated rice accessions; phylogenetic/population genomic analysis using 1,453 rice accessions from published dataset; Plant Public RNA-Seq Database used for expression prediction; Zenodo: 10.5281/zenodo.17393750
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher OsTPS8 improves seed vigor but increases grain chalkiness, reducing appearance quality
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter haplotype/base editing of OsTPS8 can tune the tradeoff between seed vigor and grain appearance; OsTPS8IR24 useful for direct-seeded indica; combine with slender grain genes to mitigate chalkiness
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsTPS8 lacks detectable canonical TPS catalytic activity despite family annotation; natural promoter SNP at −645 affects transcriptional activation and major subspecies differentiation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qPGWC-8 encodes OsTPS8, a class II TPS family member controlling grain chalkiness and seed vigor. 2. OsTPS8 suppresses Tre6P accumulation by interacting with and inhibiting OsTPS1 rather than by catalysis itself. 3. Natural promoter variation elevates OsTPS8 expression in the IR24 haplotype, causing more chalkiness but stronger seed vigor and contributing to indica–japonica differentiation.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ASO background; OsTPS8 knockout protoplast system
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing grains; downregulated by pGluC::RNAi-OsTPS1 in endosperm
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Signal restraining OsMYBS1/α-amylase expression and starch degradation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of OsTPS1 decreases Tre6P and increases grain chalkiness with abnormal starch granules
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; LCI; co-IP with OsTPS8; OsTPS1 complex enzymatic activity assay; endosperm-specific RNAi; Tre6P quantification; chalkiness and SEM phenotyping
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced OsTPS1 elevates chalkiness due to reduced Tre6P
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid downregulating OsTPS1 when breeding for grain appearance quality; OsTPS1 activity is a leverage point for Tre6P-mediated quality control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Could not be expressed in E. coli, likely due to toxicity; enzymatic activity assessed from immunoprecipitated OsTPS1-containing complexes from protoplasts
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsTPS1 is the active TPS source for Tre6P production in the study system. 2. OsTPS8 physically interacts with OsTPS1 and inhibits its activity. 3. Endosperm RNAi of OsTPS1 phenocopies low-Tre6P high-chalkiness grains, supporting Tre6P-mediated control.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increasing/higher expression in developing grains of C50 versus ASO
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing grains; responsive to Tre6P in rice protoplasts
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provide mobilizable energy for germination but excessive degradation compromises starch granule integrity and grain appearance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream of low Tre6P to activate α-amylase expression, promoting starch degradation linked to chalkiness and seed vigor
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in grains; Tre6P treatment of protoplasts; pOsMYBS1::LUC reporter assay; knockout mutant analysis of downstream α-amylase gene expression
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tre6P treatment (150 nM; 0–120 min) in rice protoplasts; grain developmental stage
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced OsMYBS1-driven starch mobilization may improve seed vigor but worsen chalkiness
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream target to decouple vigor and grain quality by moderating α-amylase activation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Positioned downstream of Tre6P and upstream of α-amylase genes in the proposed cascade
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYBS1 expression is higher in C50 and in OsTPS8-elevated lines with lower Tre6P. 2. Tre6P represses OsMYBS1 transcript accumulation and promoter activity. 3. OsMYBS1 is required for full induction of α-amylase genes downstream of the OsTPS8–Tre6P module.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal α-1,4-glucosidic linkages in starch to initiate starch granule degradation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage reserve degradation that generates soluble sugars and causes chalkiness when overactive
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; one of multiple α-amylases contributing to degradation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy supply for seed germination and seedling establishment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endosperm-specific overexpression causes chalky/floury grains with severe starch granule pitting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pGluC-driven overexpression; grain appearance and SEM observation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in C50 grains relative to ASO
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Improved reserve mobilization may compromise grain appearance quality
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be restrained in endosperm when breeding for low chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as functional evidence that enhanced starch degradation is sufficient to generate the observed grain defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of OsAmy1A in endosperm produces chalky or floury grains. 2. The resulting starch granules contain holes and pits, linking α-amylase activity to chalkiness morphology. 3. OsAmy1A supports the model that excessive starch degradation underlies OsTPS8-dependent chalkiness.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal α-1,4-glucosidic linkages in starch granules
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Promotes reserve breakdown and soluble sugar release
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endosperm-specific overexpression causes chalky/floury grains with abnormal pitted starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pGluC-driven overexpression; SEM and grain phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression in C50 grains than ASO
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can worsen grain quality through excessive starch degradation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for suppressing chalkiness downstream of Tre6P signaling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of five α-amylase genes functionally overexpressed in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsAmy2A overexpression is sufficient to induce chalky/floury grains. 2. The phenotype includes severe starch granule surface damage. 3. This supports α-amylase-mediated starch degradation as the proximate cause of chalkiness.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes starch α-1,4 linkages to promote starch granule degradation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Generates sugar from starch reserve degradation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endosperm-specific overexpression causes chalky/floury grains and perforated starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overmobilization of starch harms appearance quality
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsAmy3A overexpression induces severe chalkiness-related starch defects. 2. The result confirms causality between α-amylase activity and starch granule perforation. 3. OsAmy3A is part of the OsTPS8–Tre6P downstream response.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ASO; C50; rice protoplasts; ASO transgenic background
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal α-1,4-glucosidic bonds in starch during reserve mobilization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Direct executor of starch degradation affecting chalkiness and sugar release
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy supply for germination and early growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its promoter is repressed by Tre6P; overexpression causes chalky/floury grains with pitted starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; pOsAmy3D::LUC transient assay with Tre6P treatment; pGluC overexpression; SEM phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tre6P treatment (0–300 nM, 120 min) in protoplasts; grain development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in C50 grains than ASO; reduced in OsMYBS1 knockout grains
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher expression increases chalkiness while potentially favoring rapid germination
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream node to modulate starch mobilization without directly altering OsTPS8
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most directly assayed α-amylase gene in Tre6P-responsive promoter tests
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tre6P directly suppresses OsAmy3D promoter activity in protoplast assays. 2. OsAmy3D expression is elevated in the low-Tre6P/high-OsTPS8 state. 3. Overexpression causes severe chalky/floury grain phenotypes, making it a key effector of starch degradation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal α-1,4-glucosidic linkages in starch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Promotes starch reserve breakdown and sugar release
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endosperm-specific overexpression causes chalky/floury grains with severe pitting of starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in C50 grains than ASO
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotes starch loss and poor appearance quality when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for suppression to reduce chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among α-amylases sufficient to phenocopy starch degradation defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsAmy3E overexpression is sufficient to produce chalky/floury grains. 2. Starch granules develop severe holes and pits under overexpression. 3. Supports α-amylases as causal executors of the OsTPS8-dependent phenotype.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in developing grains with increasing transcript levels during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsTPS8 promoter harboring the −645 G-derived CAAGTG/CANNTG motif
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsTPS8 transcription when the functional promoter motif is present; little/no activation of the ASO promoter lacking the motif
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevates OsTPS8 expression, thereby promoting low Tre6P, higher starch degradation, and increased grain chalkiness
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; Y1H; EMSA; transient dual-LUC assays; endosperm-specific overexpression in C50; targeted base editing of OsTPS8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing the OsTPS8 promoter motif or modulating OsbHLH001 activity can tune chalkiness and seed vigor tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsbHLH001 binds a promoter motif created by the −645 A/G variation in OsTPS8. 2. It transcriptionally activates the IR24/C50 OsTPS8 promoter but not the ASO promoter. 3. Overexpression of OsbHLH001 increases OsTPS8 expression and grain chalkiness, establishing it as the upstream regulator of the cascade.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly controls Tre6P-mediated starch degradation and seed vigor
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGluC::OsbHLH001; pOsTPS8::LUC reporters of different haplotypes/mutants
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The decisive natural variant is the −645 A/G SNP that creates an extra bHLH-binding CANNTG motif in the OsTPS8IR24 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key upstream transcriptional switch explaining haplotype-dependent OsTPS8 expression and subspecies divergence in chalkiness/seed vigor
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of Tre6P homeostasis and downstream starch degradation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation worsens grain appearance through increased chalkiness while likely favoring seed vigor

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in C50 developing grains; responsive to low Tre6P status
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Tre6P negatively regulates its promoter activity and transcript accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: α-amylase genes including OsAmy3D and other α-amylase family members
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates α-amylase gene expression downstream of low Tre6P
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch degradation, contributing to chalkiness and reserve mobilization for seed vigor
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in ASO/C50 and OsTPS8 lines; Tre6P treatment with RT-qPCR and promoter-LUC; knockout mutant analysis of α-amylase gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tre6P treatment in protoplasts; grain development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to dampen chalkiness by limiting α-amylase activation downstream of Tre6P perturbation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYBS1 is repressed by Tre6P at both transcript and promoter activity levels. 2. Low-Tre6P conditions caused by elevated OsTPS8 are associated with increased OsMYBS1 expression. 3. OsMYBS1 is needed for normal induction of α-amylase genes, placing it between Tre6P and starch degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly governs starch breakdown and soluble sugar release for germination
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although downstream in the cascade, it is experimentally tested and functions as a regulatory node rather than merely a marker
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Immediate transcriptional activator linking Tre6P signaling to α-amylase-mediated starch degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation downstream of Tre6P repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher activity may aid germination but damage grain appearance
